title: ammoselinum rosengurtii
intro: Ammoselinum rosengurtii, also known as Rosengurt's ammoselinum, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, where it grows in moist meadows and along streams. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. Ammoselinum rosengurtii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
